title: Committed review catches update branches that skip concurrent field changes
description: A committed-review scenario where an update function takes a special-case branch for one field change, performs a PUT-like replacement for that field, and returns immediately even though other updatable fields can change in the same plan. The correct review should flag that branch as a real update bug because concurrent field changes are silently skipped.

scope.notes: Synthetic fixture models the partial-update bug where a field-specific update branch returns early and bypasses other changed fields.

mustCatch.description: The committed review should flag the update path because handling one changed field returns before other changed updatable fields are applied.

mustNotFlag.description: The review must not assume a special-case PUT or PATCH branch is safe when it returns early and other updatable fields may also be dirty in the same plan.

notes: Use this case to benchmark that committed review catches early-return update paths which silently skip concurrent field changes instead of treating them as harmless implementation detail.
